models.name: customLocationFindTargetResourceGroupResult

properties.name: matchedResourceSyncRule
properties.doc: The matching resource sync rule is the particular resource sync rule that matched the match expressions and labels and had lowest priority. This is the rule responsible for mapping the target resource to the target resource group.

properties.name: targetResourceGroup
properties.doc: The target resource group of matching resource sync rule. The labels from the request will be used to find out matching resource sync rule against the selector property of the resource sync rule. The one with highest priority will be returned if there are multiple matching rules.

properties.name: properties
properties.doc: The set of properties specific to a Resource Sync Rule

type.name: resourceSyncRuleProperties
type.doc: Properties for a resource sync rule. For an unmapped custom resource, its labels will be used to find out matching resource sync rules using the selector property of the resource sync rule. If this resource sync rule has highest priority among all matching rules, then the unmapped custom resource will be projected to the target resource group associated with this resource sync rule.

properties.name: priority
properties.doc: Priority represents a priority of the Resource Sync Rule

properties.name: provisioningState
properties.doc: Provisioning State for the Resource Sync Rule.

properties.name: selector
properties.doc: A label selector is composed of two parts, matchLabels and matchExpressions. The first part, matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is 'key', the operator is 'In', and the values array contains only 'value'. The second part, matchExpressions is a list of resource selector requirements. Valid operators include In, NotIn, Exists, and DoesNotExist. The values set must be non-empty in the case of In and NotIn. The values set must be empty in the case of Exists and DoesNotExist. All of the requirements, from both matchLabels and matchExpressions must all be satisfied in order to match.

type.name: ResourceSyncRulePropertiesSelector
type.doc: A label selector is composed of two parts, matchLabels and matchExpressions. The first part, matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is 'key', the operator is 'In', and the values array contains only 'value'. The second part, matchExpressions is a list of resource selector requirements. Valid operators include In, NotIn, Exists, and DoesNotExist. The values set must be non-empty in the case of In and NotIn. The values set must be empty in the case of Exists and DoesNotExist. All of the requirements, from both matchLabels and matchExpressions must all be satisfied in order to match.

properties.name: matchExpressions
properties.doc: MatchExpressions is a list of resource selector requirements. Valid operators include In, NotIn, Exists, and DoesNotExist. The values set must be non-empty in the case of In and NotIn. The values set must be empty in the case of Exists and DoesNotExist.

valueType.name: matchExpressionsProperties
valueType.doc: Resource Sync Rules matchExpression property definition.

properties.name: key
properties.doc: Key is the label key that the selector applies to.

properties.name: operator
properties.doc: The Operator field represents a key's relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

properties.name: values
properties.doc: The label value

properties.name: matchLabels
properties.doc: MatchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is 'key', the operator is 'In', and the values array contains only 'value'.

properties.name: targetResourceGroup
properties.doc: For an unmapped custom resource, its labels will be used to find matching resource sync rules. If this resource sync rule is one of the matching rules with highest priority, then the unmapped custom resource will be projected to the target resource group associated with this resource sync rule. The user creating this resource sync rule should have write permissions on the target resource group and this write permission will be validated when creating the resource sync rule.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: Resource Sync Rule name.
